compiled_state.author: Claude

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: DESIGN NOTE (from repo-snapshot-isolation brainstrom, cross-ref): the rebar MCP server's content-cache janitor uses flock-based locking (gc/lock interlock + optional per-entry reader/writer locks). flock is UNRELIABLE on NFS — fine on local disk / EBS / instance-store, but if the MCP server's snapshot cache (REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR) ever lands on EFS (NFS-backed) in the AWS provisioning, switch the cache locking to link(2)-based locking (or keep the cache on a local/EBS volume). Applies to the snapshot-isolation epic's S2 (cache+janitor). Decide cache volume placement (local/EBS vs EFS) when provisioning the MCP host.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — T3 (Integration feasibility) on plan "Docs: private-repo credentials, descriptive errors, ref/source semantics, HMAC trust model":

T3 is an OVERLAY that applies only when a plan integrates an external API/CLI/service/library or asserts a capability it has not used before. This plan is documentation-only — its Scope explicitly excludes code ("Out: code (S1-S5)"). It describes behaviors owned by the S1-S5 implementation tickets (git fetch/credential-helper requirement, ref/source semantics, HMAC trust model, REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR knobs) rather than integrating any new external capability itself. The git fetch + credential-helper/deploy-key/token mechanics are standard git, and the EFS/NFS flock caveat is already grounded in this epic's existing design note (cross-ref intact). No asserted-but-unverified external capability is introduced by the docs work.

Verdict for T3: PASS (not-applicable — docs deliverable, no integration).

Findings: none.
Affirmations: T3 (integration feasibility) — not-applicable; no external API/CLI/service integration or new capability assertion in this documentation chunk.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PASS 1 (T3 Integration feasibility): PASS — not-applicable. This is a docs-only ticket (Scope: Out = code S1-S5) describing the existing, already-used `git fetch origin` integration (see src/rebar/_reads.py:11). No new external API/CLI/service is being integrated, and auth/credential preconditions (credential helper / deploy key / token) are explicitly called out as a documentation deliverable. No findings.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 plan-review (criterion T1 — prior-art / novel-architecture justification) for the cross-referenced docs plan "Docs: private-repo credentials, descriptive errors, ref/source semantics, HMAC trust model" (xref'd here via the EFS/NFS flock caveat in AC5). Recorded here as the addressable anchor ticket.

AFFIRMATIONS (T1 — these already pass):
- Prior-art-aware attestation framing: the plan documents "the in-toto Statement shape" rather than inventing a bespoke attestation envelope — it aligns the HMAC attestation to an established standard (in-toto). This is exactly the anti-reinvention posture T1(a)/(b) asks for.
- Trust-model limits stated against the established property set: the plan explicitly documents that HMAC gives "integrity + intra-domain authenticity only; no non-repudiation / public verifiability / transparency" — i.e. it consciously scopes its divergence from the fuller signature/transparency model (in-toto/sigstore-style), which is the Rule-of-Three / anti-repackaging justification T1(b) wants.
- No reinvention of crypto machinery: the HMAC signing surface already exists and is documented (src/rebar/signing.py; docs/reuse-surface.md §1) — this ticket documents/reuses it, it does not introduce a parallel signing path. (Verified: HMAC-SHA256 over a canonical (ticket_id, manifest) payload, environment-keyed, head_sha freshness binding.)

FINDINGS (T1):
- [ ] Pin the exact in-toto Statement spec the doc claims conformance to. The plan promises to document "the in-toto Statement shape" but does not name WHICH in-toto Statement/predicate version (e.g. Statement v0.1 vs v1, and the `predicateType` used) the HMAC attestation maps onto — for an authoritative trust-model doc this is a load-bearing, unresolved standard-conformance assertion (T1(c)). Recommend the doc state the precise in-toto spec version + predicate type it conforms to (or explicitly state it borrows only the Statement *shape* and is NOT a conformant in-toto attestation, given it uses HMAC rather than the in-toto signing/DSSE envelope).
  - criteria: [T1]
  - location: plan "What" bullet 4 / AC4 ("the in-toto Statement shape are documented")
  - evidence: plan phrase "the in-toto Statement shape"; ABSENCE of a named in-toto Statement/predicate version; codebase fact — attestation is HMAC-SHA256 (signing.py:51), not a DSSE/signature envelope, so an explicit "shape-only, not conformant" caveat prevents an over-claim.
  - suggested_fix: In the HMAC trust-model doc, add a line of the form: "The attestation borrows the in-toto Statement <vX> shape (predicateType `<...>`) but is signed with environment-keyed HMAC-SHA256, not a DSSE/in-toto signature envelope — so it is shape-compatible, not a conformant in-toto attestation (consistent with the documented limits: no non-repudiation / public verifiability)."

No other T1 findings — the credential / fail-closed / ref-source-semantics content does not cross a novel-architecture or unverified-capability bright-line (credential helpers/deploy keys/tokens are well-trodden git-fetch prior art needing no prior-art search).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 plan-review (criterion G6 — approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection) for the cross-referenced docs plan "Docs: private-repo credentials, descriptive errors, ref/source semantics, HMAC trust model" (xref'd here via the EFS/NFS flock caveat in AC5; recorded here as the addressable anchor).

Verdict: PASS. Findings: none.

AFFIRMATIONS (G6):
- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS: the proposed mechanism — update the existing MCP-setup docs in place to describe the four behavior areas (private-repo fetch credential, descriptive credential-failure + fail-closed-vs-local semantics, ref/source semantics + origin/main default, HMAC trust model + limits + in-toto Statement shape, plus REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR/disk-cap/flock knobs) — is logic-complete for a documentation deliverable. There is no ordering/atomicity/concurrency surface in docs work; the only correctness risk is documenting behavior the code does not actually have, and the plan scopes the behavior areas to those owned by the existing S1-S5 implementation tickets and the already-existing HMAC signing surface (src/rebar/signing.py: HMAC-SHA256 over canonical (ticket_id, manifest), environment-keyed, head_sha freshness binding).
- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: solves the named problem (operators/agents need clear guidance + clear failures once the snapshot model requires the server to fetch from origin), not a proxy. Each AC maps 1:1 to a Why/What concern.
- APPROACH SELECTION: I generated two structurally-different alternatives and judged the chosen approach defensible. (1) Fold the credential/trust-model content into code-level docstrings / --help only rather than the setup docs — REJECTED for me to coach: operators standing up a server need standalone setup-doc guidance, not docstrings, so the plan's setup-doc placement is correct. (2) Create a brand-new dedicated trust-model doc page divorced from the setup flow — not clearly superior; the plan's choice to extend the existing setup docs + cross-ref existing artifacts (jira-sync-setup, alto-fruit-punch, signing.py) keeps blast-radius small and aligns with the codebase's existing doc topology (docs/jira-sync-setup.md, docs/config.md). The chosen approach is at-least-as-good on codebase-alignment, blast-radius, and simplicity. Alternatives discarded; not written into the plan.
- POSITIVE RATIONALE: present — the "Why" section states the chosen approach's rationale (the snapshot model adds a fetch-credential operational requirement the old "read the local checkout" model lacked, so operators/agents need documented guidance and failures). G6(4) satisfied.

ANTI-FP note: docs-only deliverable with a forced, well-understood approach — no manufactured alternatives. No G6 findings.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PASS 1 review (T11 — Data-migration / backfill safety): NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS. This is a documentation-only plan (updates README.md, docs/jira-sync-setup.md, MCP setup docs) that explicitly scopes code out ("Out: code (S1-S5)"). No schema/persisted-format change and no data backfill is introduced, so the migration-execution overlay does not apply. No findings.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 plan-review (criterion G6 — approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection) for the cross-referenced docs plan "Docs: private-repo credentials, descriptive errors, ref/source semantics, HMAC trust model" (xref'd here via the EFS/NFS flock caveat in AC5; recorded here as the addressable anchor).

Verdict: PASS. Findings: none.

AFFIRMATIONS (G6):
- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS: the mechanism — update the named MCP-setup docs in place (README.md MCP-setup section, docs/jira-sync-setup.md) to describe the four behavior areas + the new env/knobs, each pinned to a named grep-able file — is logic-complete for a documentation deliverable. Docs work has no ordering/atomicity/concurrency surface; the sole correctness risk is documenting behavior the code lacks, and the plan mitigates it by (a) scoping all code Out (S1-S5) and (b) anchoring the trust-model content to the already-shipped HMAC surface (verified: src/rebar/signing.py — HMAC-SHA256, environment-keyed, fails CLOSED on empty/foreign key at signing.py:254-282; the limits the plan documents are already stated at README.md:317-320). The flock caveat is grounded in this epic's existing design note (cross-ref intact).
- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: solves the named problem (operators/agents need clear guidance + clear failures once the snapshot model requires the server to fetch from origin), not a proxy. Each AC maps 1:1 to a Why/What concern (credential requirement, descriptive failure + fail-closed-vs-local, ref/source + signs-vs-never-signs + verified_at_sha, HMAC trust model + in-toto Statement shape, REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR/disk-cap/flock).
- APPROACH SELECTION: I generated two structurally-different alternatives and judged the chosen approach defensible. (1) Fold the credential/trust-model content into code-level docstrings / --help only — rejected: operators standing up a server need standalone setup-doc guidance, so the plan's setup-doc placement is correct. (2) A brand-new dedicated trust-model page divorced from the setup flow — not clearly superior; extending the existing setup docs + cross-referencing existing artifacts (docs/jira-sync-setup.md, alto-fruit-punch, signing.py) keeps blast-radius small and matches the codebase's existing doc topology. The chosen approach is at-least-as-good on codebase-alignment, blast-radius, testability (grep-able ACs), and simplicity. Alternatives discarded; not written into the plan.
- POSITIVE RATIONALE: present — the "Why" section states the chosen approach's rationale (the snapshot model adds a fetch-credential operational requirement the old "read the local checkout" model lacked, so operators/agents need documented guidance + clear failures). G6(4) satisfied.

ANTI-FP note: docs-only deliverable with a forced, well-understood approach — no manufactured alternatives. No G6 findings.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
DESIGN DECISIONS + DERISK RESULTS (brainstorm + experiment spike, 2026-06-28)

Decomposed into 7 child stories S1-S7 (depends_on chain: S1<-S2<-S3<-{S4,S5}; S5<-S6; {S4,S5,S6}<-S7). S1 is the ready entry point.

== DECISIONS LOCKED (this brainstorm) ==
- Posture: a LIVE proof-of-concept that runs ALL proposed code-review mechanisms and gates EVERY commit to rebar `main`, with few developers. Priorities, in order: (1) function + certified integrity on all commits to main [non-negotiable]; (2) decreased cost within AWS; (3) reliability/restore-speed/reusability ONLY if it doesn't hurt (1) or (2).
- Outage posture: FREEZE-AND-RESTORE, no break-glass in v1. If Gerrit/MCP/LLM is down, `main` is unmergeable until restored; engineering goes into fast rebuild-from-IaC + monitoring, not a bypass. Justified because GitHub mirrors all MERGED code (only in-flight reviews are at risk on a crash -> RPO acceptable; RTO via IaC).
- Hosting: AWS (account 896586841071, us-east-1) — justified by the existing company account + the flexibility to co-host the rebar MCP + avoiding not-worth-it IT approval. Cost-lean: single Graviton t4g.large, default VPC, NO ALB/NAT, gp3, SSM Parameter Store (free) over Secrets Manager, on-box nginx+Let's Encrypt.
- Reverse proxy = nginx+certbot, NOT Caddy (Caddy URL-decodes %2F by default; Gerrit needs encoded-slash preservation). One real change from the first sketch.
- Review bot = EXTERNAL bot (the existing rebar MCP) driving Gerrit's Set Review REST endpoint — not an in-process Java plugin (prior art: amarula/reviewai + official plugins/ai-code-review for the voting model; we use the external shape since the MCP already runs the kernel).
- Replication = core Apache-2.0 `replication` plugin (push), non-force, mirror=false, replicatePermissions=false. The GerritForge BSL re-license (2025-09-30) touches only pull-replication/multi-site, not this.
- GitHub lockdown = REPO-LEVEL ruleset (navapbc is a `free` org -> admin-proof ORG-level rulesets need a paid plan). AC re "no human/admin bypass" is therefore realized as: no direct/accidental pushes; TRUSTED repo admins CAN deliberately edit/disable the repo-level rule — an explicitly accepted risk for this PoC. (Refines AC4.)
- Domain: rebar.solutions.navateam.com (Gerrit + MCP on one host, nginx path-routed behind one cert).
- Infra lives under infra/ in the rebar repo and MUST be excluded from all distribution channels (sdist/wheel/Homebrew/MCP registry).
- Scope seam with b744: d251 owns the PROVEN PIPE (provision + Gerrit config + replication + lockdown + MCP casting a real LLM-Review vote + infra-level e2e proof). b744-WS6 swaps in the full four-pass reviewer + the real dogfood, plugging into this seam. d251 is independently completable.

== DERISK EXPERIMENTS (real AWS CLI as root + local Docker + gh) ==
- E1 (S1) AWS readiness: default VPC vpc-01fb22d3dd50a364c exists; AL2023 arm64 AMI via SSM param /aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-latest/al2023-ami-kernel-default-arm64; t4g.large in all us-east-1 AZs; `run-instances --dry-run` of the exact shape (t4g.large/gp3-encrypted/IMDSv2) = "Request would have succeeded" (perms + call valid).
- E2 (S1) DNS: PUBLIC hosted zone solutions.navateam.com EXISTS in this account (Z05558453EZPQLHKC20IQ) -> create rebar.solutions.navateam.com A record there. Critical assumption validated.
- E3 (S1) Secrets: SSM SecureString put -> get --with-decryption -> delete validated (secrets-at-boot pattern), no lingering param.
- E4 (S2/S3) Gerrit: official image boots on ARM64 at tag 3.14.1 (bare minor tags 3.14/3.13 DO NOT exist on Docker Hub -> pin a patch tag). Our submit-requirement predicate `label:LLM-Review=MAX AND -has:unresolved` PARSES (HTTP 200) against live Gerrit 3.14.1's query API, as do has:unresolved / -has:unresolved / label:X=MAX. nginx %2F-safe reverse-proxy config (proxy_pass with no path) passes `nginx -t`.
- E5 (S6) GitHub: navapbc/rebar is public, Issues already off, 0 deploy keys, I have admin; org plan = FREE (repo-level rulesets only). An EXISTING ruleset `main-protection` (id 18048287: pull_request + required_status_checks + deletion + non_fast_forward, admin bypass-via-PR) is INCOMPATIBLE with the mirror lock and must be REPLACED (can't both require human PRs and allow only the deploy key). Ruleset REST payload shape for the DeployKey bypass (actor_id:null, bypass_mode:always; rules update/creation/deletion/non_fast_forward) confirmed against the doc-verified enum.

== ACCEPTED RISKS (on record) ==
single box / no HA; repo-level ruleset editable by trusted repo admins; freeze-on-outage with no break-glass; in-flight reviews lost on a crash (merged code safe on GitHub).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PLAN-REVIEW REMEDIATION (Round-11, 2026-06-29) — disposition summary (full resolutions in the epic '## Round-11 remediation' section; derisk evidence in session_log a7e2-381c-6dc6-4d0c EXPERIMENT 2).

RESOLVED in Round-11 section + child edits: adapter-seam contradiction (now code_review_decision(diff_text, repo_root, ref)→{decision}, named module, co-located fail-closed rule, covering AC on S4b); webhook-carries-no-diff (PROVEN; S4b base64-decode AC); outbound read credential (bot HTTP token doubles as REST + git-over-HTTPS read; S1 scaffolds, S4a provisions); SSM alert-endpoint + bot-token scaffolding (S1); EC2-role / gerrit.config / DLM ownership contracts; BSL citation (S5 ADR-0010); events-log backfill smoke-check (S4a); plan-before-apply discipline (epic-level + S7 runbook).

ALREADY-ADDRESSED (kept, with justification — not silently ignored):
- S6-pre snapshot FORM (T4 major): dcea already specifies a machine-readable JSON ruleset export ( via gh api, 'sufficient to recreate on rollback') — the finding's concern is already met; no change needed.
- copyCondition NO_CODE_CHANGE empirical validation (T2/T3 major): S3 (a88d) AC3 already requires verifying BOTH directions on live 3.14.1 (message-only amend carries the vote; a real rebase drops it). That IS the empirical probe the finding asks for; reaffirmed.
- Rollback drill / freeze-restore (T12 major): S7 already has a restore drill on a throwaway instance (≤2h RTO) and S6 a <15-min ruleset rollback from the S6-pre snapshot; the 'drill' concern is covered by S7's restore-drill AC. Reaffirmed; the ruleset-rollback rehearsal is folded into S6's documented <15-min procedure.
- Monitoring metrics/alarms undefined (T10 major): S7 already defines a concrete custom metric (Rebar/Gate/GerritReachable), the alarm (<1 for 2 periods, treat_missing_data=breaching), the EC2 StatusCheckFailed backstop, and SNS→SSM endpoint. The finding's 'no concrete metrics/alarms' reads against the lean epic body, but the child S7 ACs specify them; reaffirmed.
- gerrit config DRIFT management (T10 major): addressed by the gerrit.config ownership contract (S2 base + S3 [change]-only RMW) + infra-as-code under infra/; configs are rendered/committed, not hand-edited, so terraform/compose is the drift source of truth.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
CORRECTION of the prior comment (a few file/metric tokens were dropped by a shell backtick artifact). Clean disposition of d251's findings — full resolutions live in the epic "## Round-11 remediation" section; derisk evidence in session_log a7e2-381c-6dc6-4d0c (EXPERIMENT 2).

RESOLVED in the Round-11 section + child edits: adapter-seam contradiction (now code_review_decision(diff_text, repo_root, ref) -> {decision: PASS|BLOCK}, named module src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py, co-located fail-closed rule, covering AC on S4b/918c); webhook-carries-no-diff (PROVEN — receiver fetches GET /a/changes/{id}/revisions/{rev}/patch and base64-decodes; S4b AC); outbound read credential (bot HTTP token doubles as REST + git-over-HTTPS read; S1/5bfc scaffolds, S4a/42ba provisions); SSM scaffolding adds /rebar/prod/alert-endpoint + /rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token (S1); EC2-role / gerrit.config / DLM ownership contracts; BSL citation (S5/4e82 ADR-0010); events-log backfill smoke-check (S4a); plan-before-apply discipline (epic + S7 runbook).

ALREADY-ADDRESSED (kept, with justification — not silently ignored):
- S6-pre snapshot FORM (T4): dcea already specifies a machine-readable JSON ruleset export (infra/github/main-protection.snapshot.json via gh api, "sufficient to recreate on rollback") — concern already met.
- copyCondition NO_CODE_CHANGE empirical validation (T2/T3): S3/a88d AC3 already requires verifying BOTH directions live on 3.14.1 (message-only amend carries the vote; a real rebase drops it). That IS the empirical probe.
- Rollback drill (T12): S7/3178 has a restore drill on a throwaway instance (<=2h RTO); S6/075f a <15-min ruleset rollback from the S6-pre snapshot. Covered.
- Monitoring metrics/alarms (T10): S7 already defines the custom metric (Rebar/Gate/GerritReachable), the alarm (value <1 for 2 periods, treat_missing_data=breaching), the EC2 StatusCheckFailed backstop, and SNS->SSM endpoint. The finding read against the lean epic body; the child ACs specify them.
- gerrit config DRIFT (T10): addressed by the gerrit.config ownership contract (S2 base + S3 [change]-only read-modify-write) + IaC under infra/; configs are rendered/committed, not hand-edited.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: FORCE_CLOSE: close gate(s) bypassed by user approval — no completion/signature attestation was signed. Reason: "All 10 children closed (S1,S2,S3,S4a,S5,S4b,S6-pre,S6,S7,S8). The ENTIRE topology was built + proven LIVE end-to-end on the running AWS box (i-00880b2c7f13527c5, rebar.solutions.navateam.com): provision (S1) -> Gerrit+review-bot behind nginx/TLS (S2) -> LLM-Review label+submit-req (S3) -> bot identity+webhooks+events-log (S4a) -> review_code->LLM-Review vote, the proven pipe + /rerun (S4b, change #101 PASS: vote==MAX->submit->MERGED->replicated to GitHub) -> Gerrit->GitHub non-force replication via deploy key (S5) -> the deploy-key-only GitHub ruleset (S6, APPLIED + EMPIRICALLY PROVEN to reject human push/force-push/PR-merge(--admin)/tag/deletion) -> e2e + monitoring(SNS delivery proven) + quiesced restore drill (RTO ~126s) + runbooks (S7) -> client walkthrough (S8). The 2 unmet epic ACs (repo PERMANENTLY locked; e2e on  with permanent direct-push rejection) are the DELIBERATE consequence of the operator's explicit apply-prove-rollback choice (confirmed via question): the lock + main-advance were proven then rolled back so the team's live repo/workflow is unchanged; the e2e ran on a deletable test branch + the lock-rejection was proven transiently in S6. The full permanent-cutover playbook (github_repository_ruleset IaC + apply/rollback scripts, feature toggles, mirror banner, Gerrit-only CONTRIBUTING workflow) is committed in infra/ + the runbooks for the real cutover. Closed-without-signature reflects the intentionally-deferred permanent cutover, not a capability gap.". Session: 19ba620e.

compiled_state.description:
# Provision AWS-hosted Gerrit + rebar MCP; lock rebar's GitHub `main` to Gerrit

## Context
We are adopting Gerrit as the gatekeeper for all code merged to rebar's `main`, self-hosted on AWS alongside the rebar MCP server, with GitHub as a downstream read-only mirror (the Go-project topology). This epic stands up that environment and locks the rebar GitHub repo so `main` only advances via commits Gerrit replicates after its submit requirements pass. It is the prerequisite that enables dogfooding the agentic code-review capability on rebar itself.

## Success Criteria
- A single AWS instance hosts Gerrit (NoteDb, no external DB) + the rebar MCP server.
- rebar's GitHub `main` can only be updated by Gerrit's replication identity; all other pushes / PR-merges / force-pushes are rejected.
- An `LLM-Review` label + submit requirement are wired to the rebar MCP server, and a change flows end-to-end (push to Gerrit -> review -> submit -> replicate to GitHub).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] AWS instance (~8 GB; e.g. t4g.large) provisioned; Gerrit + rebar MCP each containerized; Gerrit heap pinned (~2 GB); reverse proxy on 443; Gerrit HTTP 8080 + SSH 29418.
- [ ] Gerrit rebar project configured: `change.submitWholeTopic=true`; custom `LLM-Review` label; submit requirement = the SINGLE deterministic vote `label:LLM-Review=MAX AND -has:unresolved` (the LLM-Review label is voted by the deterministic pass-3 blocker based on thresholds; there is exactly ONE code-review vote, with no separate human Code-Review or CI Verified votes); `copyCondition` carrying the vote across trivial rebases.
- [ ] Gerrit replication plugin pushes `main` -> GitHub (non-force), authenticated by a dedicated single-repo write **deploy key** as the replication identity (see S5). (Supersedes the earlier 'GitHub App / service account' phrasing: a deploy key is the simpler fit for one repo, and is the sole ruleset bypass actor in S6.)
- [ ] GitHub rebar repo locked: ruleset restricts `main` updates to the replication identity only (block force-push + deletions); repo write limited to that identity; no human/admin bypass.
- [ ] rebar MCP server reachable by Gerrit (stream-events/webhook) and can vote `LLM-Review` via REST.
- [ ] End-to-end proof: a test change is reviewed + submitted in Gerrit and appears on GitHub `main`; a direct GitHub push / PR-merge to `main` is rejected.


## Decomposition & resolved design questions (2026-06-28 brainstorm + derisk + peer review)
Decomposed into **9 stories**: S1 (TF base), S2 (Gerrit + review-bot receiver + nginx/TLS), S3
(project/label/submit-req/topic), S4a (bot identity + event plumbing), S4b (receiver review/vote
logic + backfill), S5 (replication), S6-pre (mirror-lock pre-work), S6 (apply mirror lock), S7
(e2e + backup/restore + monitoring). The plan-review gaps on the original ACs are resolved as:
- **IaC tooling + remote state:** Terraform with an S3 backend (`use_lockfile`, TF >= 1.10) + a
  one-time state-bucket bootstrap — S1.
- **Secrets management:** AWS SSM Parameter Store SecureString + a least-privilege instance-role
  policy, pulled to a `chmod 600` `.env` at boot — S1/S2 (scoping reinforced in S4a).
- **rebar MCP is stdio-only -> Gerrit can't reach it:** S2 stands up a thin **webhook->review->vote
  RECEIVER** (a plain HTTP service that IMPORTS the review kernel as a library) behind nginx at
  `/review/`; the stdio MCP is NOT exposed over HTTP (Gerrit POSTs webhooks, not MCP — an MCP HTTP
  transport would reject the webhook). S4a wires the bot identity/plugins/inbound auth; S4b the
  review/vote logic.
- **Gerrit event mechanism:** the `webhooks` plugin (push) + the `events-log` plugin with a
  reconciler poller for backfill — S4a/S4b. (Not SSH stream-events.) NOTE: the `webhooks` plugin
  has NO native HMAC, so inbound auth is a secret-token URL + network ACL (S4a), not a signature.
- **NoteDb persistence + backup:** NoteDb on a dedicated gp3 EBS volume at `/var/gerrit` with
  `prevent_destroy` + DLM daily snapshots (S1) and a non-destructive restore drill (S7); merged
  code is always safe on the GitHub mirror.
- **gate verdict -> `LLM-Review` integer mapping:** the gate's `finalize_verdict` emits
  `PASS`/`BLOCK`/`INDETERMINATE`; the voter maps `PASS`=+MAX, `BLOCK`/`INDETERMINATE`=-1
  (fail-closed), config-driven — S4b.

**AC4 refinement:** the lockdown is a REPO-LEVEL ruleset (navapbc is a `free` org; admin-proof
org-level rulesets need a paid plan). So "no human/admin bypass" means no direct/accidental
pushes + force-push/deletion blocked (proven empirically: an active deploy-key-only ruleset
rejects an admin push); TRUSTED repo admins can deliberately edit/disable the rule — an
explicitly accepted PoC risk. The existing `main-protection` ruleset (PR + status-checks) is
replaced by the mirror lock (S6).

**Peer-review corrections (folded into the children):** (a) S6 uses `terraform-provider-github`
>= 6.8.0's NATIVE DeployKey bypass (the prior `null_resource`/`gh api`/two-phase dance is
dropped); `actor_id:null` is canonical (empirically confirmed). (b) S4 split into S4a/S4b and
reframed to a webhook receiver (not MCP-over-HTTP); inbound auth via secret-URL + ACL (the
plugin has no HMAC); async ACK + idempotency vs `webhooks` retries. (c) S3 copyCondition is
`changekind:NO_CODE_CHANGE` only (dropping `TRIVIAL_REBASE`, which would certify a moved-base
tree). (d) S5 adds a `projects=` single-repo binding (a functional bug otherwise) + a crash-safe
`replication.eventsDirectory`.

## Plan-review remediation notes (recalibrated gate)
- **Rollback / availability (T4):** replacing `main-protection` is reversible — S6 documents a <15-min rollback (ruleset `enforcement=disabled`, restore `main-protection` from the S6-pre snapshot, re-enable PRs/Issues/Actions). Outage posture is the locked **freeze-and-restore** (no break-glass v1): if Gerrit/the receiver/LLM is down, `main` freezes until restored; merged code is always safe on the GitHub mirror.
- **Architecture decisions captured (T5e):** recorded as ADRs in the children — 0007-review-bot-receiver + 0008-secrets-source-ssm (S2), 0009-review-bot-pipe (S4b), 0010-gerrit-github-replication (S5), 0011-github-mirror-lock (S6-pre), 0012-iac-foundations (S1). CLAUDE.md is updated when d251 lands.
- **Integration de-risked up-front, not only at S7 (G5):** the two riskiest seams were validated empirically before build (session_log vivid-mull-need): (a) an active repo-level ruleset with deploy-key-only bypass REJECTS a non-bypass admin push (proven on the real repo, GH013); (b) the DeployKey bypass `actor_id:null` is accepted + canonical; (c) Gerrit 3.14.1 boots on arm64 and the submit-requirement predicate + nginx %2F config validate. S7 remains the full e2e proof, but the high-risk mechanisms are already de-risked.
- **What S4b's "proven pipe" actually reviews (G6 seam):** the d251 receiver casts the `LLM-Review` vote by invoking the EXISTING `rebar.llm.review_code(...)` (single-pass, ships today) — clean->label MAX, issues/error->-1 fail-closed — proving the webhook->review->vote->submit->replicate plumbing. The REAL four-pass code-review gate (criteria/prompts/context via `gate_dispatch.produce_code_review_verdict`) is **b744-WS6**, which swaps into the same seam for the dogfood. d251 proves the pipe; b744 supplies the review depth. (Note: this is CODE review — verdict PASS|BLOCK — not the plan-review gate's `finalize_verdict`.)

## Round-7 remediation
- **Proven-pipe decision rule (G6):** `rebar.llm.review_code()` returns a `review_result` (a `findings` list), NOT a verdict string. The receiver derives a binary decision: **BLOCK if any finding is at/above a configured blocking severity (default critical/high), else PASS** -> PASS->label MAX, BLOCK or error/no-result->-1 (fail-closed); the threshold is config-driven. b744-WS6 will ADD a code-review verdict producer to `gate_dispatch` (planned — e.g. `produce_code_review_verdict` returning a typed `code_review_verdict {verdict: PASS|BLOCK, ...}`); it does NOT exist yet (today `gate_dispatch` exports only `produce_plan_review_verdict` / `produce_completion_verdict`), so until b744 lands the pipe uses `review_code`'s findings + the decision rule above.

## Round-9 remediation
- **Formal d251->b744 seam contract (T5e):** the receiver depends on a stable ADAPTER interface — `code_review_decision(diff_text) -> {decision: PASS|BLOCK}` (name TBD) — NOT on `review_code`/`produce_*` directly. d251 implements the adapter over `review_code()`'s findings (the severity-threshold rule); b744-WS6 reimplements the SAME adapter over its four-pass verdict producer. The swap is drop-in with no receiver change. That interface IS the formal seam.


## Round-11 remediation (2026-06-29 — derisk experiment + coherence)
Derisk evidence: session_log a7e2-381c-6dc6-4d0c (EXPERIMENT 2 — live REST contract against gerrit-review.googlesource.com).

- **Adapter seam reconciled (supersedes Round-9's `diff_text`-only contract — G4 critical):** the
  formal seam is `code_review_decision(diff_text: str, repo_root: Path, ref: str) -> {decision: PASS|BLOCK}`
  (concrete name, module `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py` — no longer "TBD"). The adapter passes **`source="local"`** to `review_code()` — the receiver has already cloned the Gerrit change ref into `repo_root`, so `review_code` must read that checkout directly; `attested` mode is WRONG here (it would `git fetch origin` against Gerrit and try to resolve the change ref as an origin branch). The third/fourth
  args are REQUIRED because `review_code()` needs a repo context (`resolve_gate_handle`), which
  `diff_text` alone cannot supply — this is exactly S4b's Round-10 reality, so Round-9 and
  Round-10 are now consistent. **Error/fallback rule co-located with the schema:** any
  error / no-result / unparseable verdict → `decision: BLOCK` (fail-closed → `-1`). The adapter return is `{decision: PASS|BLOCK, message: str, findings: [...]}` — NOT decision-only — so the receiver assembles the Gerrit vote COMMENT uniformly from `message`/`findings`; d251 fills them from `review_code()`'s findings, b744-WS6 from `produce_code_review_verdict`, preserving 'no receiver change' for the comment dimension too. d251-S4b
  (918c) implements the adapter over `review_code()`'s findings + the severity-threshold rule;
  b744-WS6 (4119) reimplements the SAME signature over `produce_code_review_verdict` — drop-in,
  no receiver change. A covering AC for the adapter is added to S4b.
- **Webhook carries NO diff (T3 critical — PROVEN):** the `webhooks`-plugin payload (and the
  change-metadata JSON) carry no diff/patch field; the receiver fetches it via a separate
  authenticated REST call `GET /a/changes/{id}/revisions/{rev}/patch` which returns a
  **base64-encoded** git format-patch (must be base64-decoded), or the JSON
  `/files/{f}/diff` (XSSI-guarded). S4b's Round-9 AC ("fetch diff via Gerrit REST as
  `diff_text=`") is clarified to require the base64 decode. The `events-log` REST backfill
  query (`GET /a/plugins/events-log/events`) was not in the named E1–E5 derisk set but IS an
  S4a AC — flagged for an explicit smoke-check there before S4b's reconciler depends on it. The reconciler is RESUMABLE+IDEMPOTENT via a persisted cursor (last-processed events-log event id) + the per-(change,revision) vote-existence check, so webhook and backfill converge to one vote; FALLBACK if events-log is absent/errors/malformed: log + trip the voter alarm + rely on webhook (degraded), NEVER mark a missed change submittable-but-unreviewed (fail-safe).
- **Outbound READ credential provisioned (G3/G4 critical):** the bot's Gerrit HTTP token
  (S4a, SSM `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token`) is the SAME credential used for both the REST
  `/a/` calls (diff fetch + vote) AND git-over-HTTPS clone of the change ref into `repo_root`.
  S1 scaffolds the param; S4a generates+stores it; documented that its scope covers both. No
  separate SSH deploy key is needed for the receiver's read path — and a per-`(change_id, revision)` SINGLE-FLIGHT lock guards the review+vote critical section so a webhook retry or backfill firing mid-review cannot spawn a second review or cast a conflicting vote against Gerrit's last-write-wins Set Review (receipt-time dedup alone is insufficient; detail on S4b) (the replication deploy key
  in S5 is write-only to GitHub, a different identity).
- **SSM scaffolding completeness (G3 major):** S1's SSM SecureString scaffolding adds
  `/rebar/prod/alert-endpoint` (consumed by S7 `monitoring.tf`) and `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token`
  (consumed by S4a) to the enumerated set, so no downstream `data.aws_ssm_parameter` lookup
  fails on a missing param.
- **IAM-role ownership contract (G4 major):** S1 CREATES the single EC2 instance role; S2 and
  S4a ADD separately-named scoped inline policies (`aws_iam_role_policy` attachments) — they
  never re-declare the role. Documented so two applies can't clobber each other's grants.
- **`gerrit.config` ownership contract (G4 major):** S2 owns the base `gerrit.config`; S3
  patches ONLY the `[change]` section via documented read-modify-write (not a full-file
  overwrite), mirroring S4a's `refs/meta/config` RMW. No story rewrites the whole file.
- **DLM lifecycle-policy ownership (G4 major):** S1 OWNS the DLM service role + the
  `aws_dlm_lifecycle_policy` (retain count=7); S7 only ASSERTS/monitors it (no second DLM
  resource) — resolves the S1/S7 double-management risk.
- **Replication-plugin BSL scope (T1 critical):** the claim that GerritForge's 2025-09-30 BSL
  re-license touches only pull-replication/multi-site (not the core Apache-2.0 `replication`
  push plugin) must be CITED in S5's ADR-0010 against GerritForge's published scope, OR
  reframed as an assumption verified by inspecting the plugin LICENSE in the pinned 3.14.1
  image before S5 builds. S5 AC updated.
- **`terraform plan`-before-`apply` discipline (T10 major):** reaffirmed as a cross-story
  rule, especially for the high-stakes S6 apply that replaces the live `main-protection`
  ruleset; already in S7's runbook AC, now stated at the epic level.
compiled_state.title: Provision AWS Gerrit + rebar MCP; lock rebar GitHub main to Gerrit (code-review dogfood prerequisite)
